channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: RK Hoops-QC Defeats Caloocan Thanks to Tantoy Ferrer's Double-Double Performance
item.description: The RK Hoops-Quezon City team pulled off a victory against Caloocan in a recent Women's Maharlika Pilipinas Basketball League (WMPBL) game, thanks to a stellar performance from Tantoy Ferrer. Ferrer d...

item.title: BBC announces significant workforce reduction of up to 2,000 roles
item.description: The BBC has announced plans to reduce its workforce by up to 2,000 positions, marking its most substantial downsizing in a decade and a half. The decision comes amid financial pressures and a shift in...

item.title: Alberta introduces app to help truckers avoid low-clearance bridges
item.description: Alberta is introducing a new app called the Canadian Trucking Regulations Hub to help commercial truck drivers plan safer routes and prevent collisions with low-clearance bridges. The app, which will ...

item.title: Liberals explore age restrictions on social media as part of broader online safety measures
item.description: The Canadian federal government is exploring measures to enhance online safety for minors, including the potential restriction of social media use for those under 16, mirroring a law in Australia. Can...

item.title: Atiku Say Jonathan’s Lack of Experience Affected His Time as President
item.description: Former Vice President of Nigeria, Atiku Abubakar, don talk say former President Goodluck Jonathan be decent man but him no too get experience for government work. Atiku yarn this one during interview ...

item.title: Parents increasingly gift money to help children enter the housing market
item.description: Recent research from Mozo highlights a significant shift in the 'Bank of Mum and Dad' phenomenon, showing that more Australian parents are now providing financial support to their children as gifts ra...

item.title: TCS Nashik harassment case: Women employees say isolated on rooftop, phones taken, police probe nine complaints

item.title: Quebec monitors multiple rivers for potential flooding as rain continues
item.description: Several rivers across Quebec are currently under flood watch due to forecasted rainfall expected to continue over the next few days. As of noon today, authorities reported a medium-level flood near Ra...

item.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor outlines conservative immigration policy sparking political backlash
item.description: Liberal Party leader Angus Taylor has unveiled a new immigration plan that seeks to reduce migration numbers and place a stronger emphasis on what he describes as 'Australian values' when deciding who...

item.title: MCL Launches Rising Woman Platform in Zanzibar to Promote Women’s Stories

item.title: Regional and Global Health Experts Meet in Nairobi to Discuss the Future of Kenya’s Healthcare System
item.description: The 53rd Annual Scientific Conference of the Kenya Medical Association (KMA) has officially opened in Nairobi, drawing together local, regional, and international medical experts to plan the future of...

item.title: Court overturns heroin conviction, frees mother and son-in-law
item.description: In Arusha, the Court of Appeal has released Maria Mtumbuka and her son-in-law, Hamis Awadhi, who were earlier sentenced to 30 years in prison for trafficking over 12 kilograms of heroin. They were arr...

item.title: Bohol’s rock-eating shipworm named Philippine entry in global mollusk competition
item.description: The University of the Philippines-Marine Science Institute (UPMSI) has announced that Bohol’s unique rock-eating shipworm, known locally as ‘antingaw’ or scientifically as Lithoredo abatanica, is the ...

item.title: Analysis of U.S. Military Engagement in Middle East Conflicts
item.description: The article discusses the complex relationship between Israel and the United States, particularly in the context of military involvement in Middle East conflicts. It highlights how U.S. foreign policy...

item.title: US to End Waiver Permitting India to Import Russian Oil
item.description: The United States Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, announced that Washington will not be renewing the sanctions waivers that allowed countries, including India, to buy Russian and Iranian oil. India...

item.title: Nepal PM Balen Shah's visit to India pending finalisation after bilateral consultations
item.description: Nepalese Prime Minister Balendra Shah, popularly known as 'Balen', is expected to visit India, but the dates and itinerary of his trip have not been finalised yet. Nepal’s Foreign Minister Shisir Khan...

item.title: Government clarifies South India will gain seats under revised Lok Sabha allocation
item.description: The central government has clarified concerns regarding the allocation of Lok Sabha seats to southern states under a new formula. According to the proposed plan, all states will see a proportional inc...

item.title: Edmonton Oilers improve defence and enter playoffs with strong prospects
item.description: After a challenging regular season, the Edmonton Oilers have transformed their performance thanks to key trade acquisitions and a renewed focus on defensive play, partly due to the absence of star for...

item.title: Australia’s Vulnerabilities Exposed by Middle East Conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is highlighting structural vulnerabilities in Australia, particularly in energy security, trade, and defence preparedness. Despite being an energy exporter, Aus...

item.title: American Cola Courtside 2026 introduces women’s basketball tournament, Team MC Spencer wins title
item.description: The 2026 edition of the American Cola Courtside basketball series made history by debuting a women’s category, marking a major step for female athletes in the competition. The event, held in Lagos, br...
